text: 6 Minute English from bbclearningenglish .com
zh: [0] 来自bbclearningenglish.com的《六分钟英语》

text: Hello. This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: [1] 大家好，欢迎收听BBC英语学习的《六分钟英语》。

text: I'm Pippa. And I'm Phil. What's the last thing you bought and why did you buy it?
zh: [2] 我是Pippa。我是Phil。你最近买的最后一件东西是什么，为什么买它？

text: I bought a new pair of headphones recently because my old ones broke and I did lots of research to try and find a good pair.
zh: [3] 我最近买了一副新耳机，因为我原来的坏了，我做了很多调查想找一副好的。

text: How about you? I bought a soft drink on the way to work this morning.
zh: [4] 你呢？我今天早上上班路上买了一瓶软饮料。

text: It might be because I saw someone I like promoting it in an advert.
zh: [5] 可能是因为我看到我喜欢的人在广告里推荐它。

text: We call this a celebrity endorsement, but why do they work?
zh: [6] 我们把这称为名人代言，但它们为什么有效呢？

text: Let's start by hearing from Ben Jones, a behavioural research expert speaking to BBC World Service programme Business Daily.
zh: [7] 我们先来听听行为研究专家Ben Jones在BBC世界服务节目《商业日报》上的说法。

text: When we're unsure, we don't know what to do, it's kind of easy to follow the lead of those who we think are credible or knowledgeable.
zh: [8] 当我们不确定该怎么做时，跟随我们认为可信或懂行的人的做法就比较容易。

text: It's a mental shortcut. It's something that that helps us navigate uncertain times or make quick choices.
zh: [9] 这是一种心理捷径，能帮助我们应对不确定的情况或快速做出选择。

text: We start doing that from an early age. Our parents, for some people, our teachers, you know, these people help us learn and make sense of the world.
zh: [10] 我们从小就开始这样做。对一些人来说，是父母；对另一些人来说，是老师——这些人帮助我们学习、理解世界。

text: And some businesses can draw on this through things like endorsements, you know, dentists recommending toothpaste, athletes endorsing sportswear.
zh: [11] 一些企业可以利用这一点，比如通过代言，像牙医推荐牙膏、运动员代言运动装备。

text: Ben says that endorsements work as a mental shortcut.
zh: [12] Ben说，代言的作用就像一种心理捷径。

text: This is something that helps us make a decision quickly. If we trust the endorsement, then we don't need to think about it that much.
zh: [13] 这种捷径能帮助我们快速做决定。如果我们相信这个代言，就不需要再多加思考。

text: In this episode, we're going to look at the way that people can be convinced to do things.
zh: [14] 在这一集里，我们要探讨人们是如何被说服去做某些事情的。

text: And, as usual, we'll learn some useful new words and phrases.
zh: [15] 和往常一样，我们也会学习一些实用的新单词和短语。

text: And remember, you'll find all the vocabulary and a quiz on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: [16] 记住，你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到所有词汇和一个小测验。

text: OK, but first I have a question for you, Pippa.
zh: [17] 好的，不过首先我有个问题要问你，Pippa。

text: Today we're talking about behavioural science.
zh: [18] 今天我们要聊聊行为科学。

text: What's the name of the theory that says people respond to small prompts?
zh: [19] 有一个理论认为人们会对小小的提示做出反应，这个理论叫什么名字？

text: Is it A push theory, B pull theory or C nudge theory?
zh: [20] 是A推动理论，B拉动理论，还是C助推理论？

text: I'm not sure. I'm going to say B pull theory. OK, well we'll find out the answer at the end of the programme.
zh: [21] 我不确定，我猜是B拉动理论。好的，我们会在节目最后揭晓答案。

text: In recent years, many fast food chains have installed touchscreen machines for people to order their food.
zh: [22] 近年来，许多快餐连锁店安装了触屏点餐机，供顾客点餐。

text: They've reported taking more money as a result.
zh: [23] 他们报告说，这样做后收入增加了。

text: Could behavioural science tell us why this happens?
zh: [24] 行为科学能解释这是为什么吗？

text: Here's product designer Dean Ward speaking to BBC World Service's Business Daily.
zh: [25] 这是产品设计师Dean Ward在BBC世界服务《商业日报》节目中的发言。

text: Firstly, the psychology of speaking to someone and feeling judged we think is a key factor. So would you like extra fry?
zh: [26] 首先，我们认为一个关键因素是：跟人说话时会有被评判的心理压力。比如，'您要加薯条吗？'

text: Would you like to go large? Not all people, but I think there's definitely a large proportion of people who may feel judged in those instances and may say no.
zh: [27] '您要加大份吗？'并不是所有人都会这样，但我认为确实有相当一部分人在这种情况下会觉得被评判，从而选择拒绝。

text: There's the fact that you've got more time to look. You can see the product, you can see what's in it,
zh: [28] 而使用机器时，你有更多时间去看：你能看到产品本身，看到里面有什么，

text: you can see other products linked to it as well. And also around upsell as well.
zh: [29] 也能看到与之关联的其他产品，还涉及到追加销售。

text: Would you like to add this? Would you like to do that? Because you've got more time and you're not being judged, you're very much more inclined to actually say yes to these things.
zh: [30] '您要加这个吗？您要加那个吗？'因为你有更多时间，也不会觉得被评判，所以你更倾向于答应这些请求。

text: And that's typically what we're seeing. An increased basket spend of typically 25 -30%.
zh: [31] 而这正是我们通常看到的结果：购物篮消费通常会增加25%到30%。

text: If people feel judged, then it means that they worry what other people will think of them.
zh: [32] 如果人们觉得被评判，就意味着他们担心别人会怎么看自己。

text: If we have to ask a human for extra fries, we might worry what they think of us.
zh: [33] 如果我们必须向一个真人要求加薯条，我们可能会担心对方怎么看我们。

text: but no one feels judged by a computer screen. If you upsell something, then you get someone to spend more.
zh: [34] 但没有人会觉得被一块电脑屏幕评判。如果你进行追加销售，就是让顾客多花钱。

text: For example, waiters often try to get people to buy larger portions, more drinks or dessert.
zh: [35] 比如，服务员常常试图让顾客点更大份的餐、更多饮料或甜点。

text: Dean says that upselling can increase people's basket spend.
zh: [36] Dean说，追加销售可以增加顾客的购物篮消费。

text: A basket spend is the total amount that someone spends in one transaction, on everything that's in their basket.
zh: [37] 购物篮消费指的是一个人在一次交易中，为购物篮里所有商品所花的总金额。

text: Now, this is an example of a business using behavioural science to nudge people to do things,
zh: [38] 这是企业利用行为科学来'助推'人们做某事的一个例子，

text: But it's not just businesses that use behavioural science in this way.
zh: [39] 但利用行为科学的不只是企业。

text: In Tunisia, the United Nations World Food Programme wanted to encourage people to eat food together more often.
zh: [40] 在突尼斯，联合国世界粮食计划署希望鼓励人们更多地一起用餐。

text: They made a TV show set in a restaurant.
zh: [41] 他们制作了一档以餐厅为背景的电视节目。

text: Let's hear from Taqwa Khalifi from the World Food Programme, talking to BBC World Service's Business Daily.
zh: [42] 我们来听听世界粮食计划署的Taqwa Khalifi在BBC世界服务《商业日报》节目中的发言。

text: So we needed something really creative and really different
zh: [43] 所以我们需要一些真正有创意、与众不同的东西，

text: to think outside of the box or to let people see themselves in those stories because
zh: [44] 需要跳出固有思维框架，让人们能在这些故事中看到自己，因为

text: people are learning and changing by imitating and modeling and
zh: [45] 人们是通过模仿和示范来学习和改变的，

text: like observing others' behaviors and others' actions and emotional reactions to things.
zh: [46] 也就是观察他人的行为、举动以及他们对事物的情绪反应。

text: So this is why we tried this project or tried this product or this TV series in order to impact people.
zh: [47] 所以我们才尝试了这个项目、这个产品，或者说这部电视剧，希望能对人们产生影响。

text: Taguas says that they needed to think outside the box.
zh: [48] Taqwa说他们需要跳出固有思维框架。

text: This means to try and think in a different way to come up with something creative.
zh: [49] 这意味着尝试用不同的方式思考，从而想出有创意的点子。

text: The TV programme leads to people modelling behaviour.
zh: [50] 这部电视节目促使人们效仿其中的行为。

text: If you model behaviour, then you behave in a way that other people can copy.
zh: [51] 如果你'示范'某种行为，就是以一种别人可以模仿的方式行事。

text: Now, Phil, I think it's time to hear the answer to your question. Yes, it is.
zh: [52] 好了，Phil，我想是时候揭晓你那个问题的答案了。是的，没错。

text: I asked what theory says that people respond to small prompts to change their behaviour?
zh: [53] 我问的是：哪种理论认为人们的行为会因小小的提示而改变？

text: I thought it was pull theory. Which I'm afraid was the wrong answer.
zh: [54] 我以为是拉动理论，恐怕这个答案是错的。

text: Nudge theory says that people's behaviour can be changed by small prompts or nudges.
zh: [55] 助推理论认为，人们的行为可以通过小小的提示或'助推'来改变。

text: OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learned, starting with mental shortcut, which is something that helps us make a decision quickly,
zh: [56] 好，我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇，首先是'心理捷径'，指帮助我们快速做决定的东西，

text: usually because we don't need to think about it. If someone feels judged, then they're worried about what other people think about them.
zh: [57] 通常是因为我们不需要多加思考。如果一个人'感觉被评判'，那就是他们担心别人怎么看自己。

text: Upsell refers to persuading someone to buy something extra, like a dessert at a restaurant.
zh: [58] 'Upsell（追加销售）'指的是说服某人多花钱买点额外的东西，比如在餐厅点份甜点。

text: Basket spend is what you spend in one transaction, on the things in your shopping basket.
zh: [59] '购物篮消费'是指你在一次交易中，为购物篮里的商品所花的钱。

text: If you think outside the box, then you think about something in a different way to come up with a creative solution.
zh: [60] 如果你'跳出固有思维框架'，就是用不同的方式思考问题，从而想出有创意的解决方案。

text: And finally, modelling is behaving in a way that others can copy.
zh: [61] 最后，'模仿示范（modelling）'指的是以一种别人可以效仿的方式行事。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up, but why not head over to our website,
zh: [62] 好了，我们的六分钟又到时间了，不过别忘了访问我们的网站，

text: site, bbclearningenglish .com, to try the quiz and worksheet for this episode. See you again soon. Bye!
zh: [63] bbclearningenglish.com，去做做这期节目的小测验和练习题。我们下次再见，拜拜！
